concerns.text: FR-007 mandates logging critical missing variables as '[NEEDS CLARIFICATION]'. This contradicts US-1 Acceptance Scenario 3 and the 'Assumptions' section, which explicitly state that missing values will be 'imputed with a default value' or 'flagged for exclusion'. The spec cannot simultaneously require a clarification log for missing data AND define a deterministic exclusion/imputation path for the same scenario.

The Independent Test for US-2 references a dataset size of '[deferred] molecules'. While '[deferred]' is a sanctioned marker for empirical values, the surrounding text defines a specific test condition ('subset of the data') that conflicts with the explicit constraint in US-2 Acceptance Scenario 1 ('≤ 5,000 molecules'). The test definition should align with the concrete constraint or explicitly defer the size limit as well to avoid ambiguity in test execution.

FR-003 states the system must avoid '8-bit quantization libraries'. This is an implementation constraint that contradicts the 'CPU-Optimized' goal in US-2 if quantization is the only way to meet the 6-hour runtime (SC-003) on the 7GB RAM runner (SC-005). The requirement forbids a potential optimization path without clarifying if the 6-hour/7GB constraints are hard limits that might necessitate such techniques, creating a potential conflict between performance goals and implementation restrictions.
